channel.title: Roswell Incident on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Roswell Incident on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Glenn Dennis
item.description:
Glenn Dennis (1925–2015) was a mortician and a founder of the International UFO Museum and Research Center in Roswell, New Mexico. He became a prominent figure in ufology in 1994, claiming to have received classified information regarding the 1947 Roswell UFO incident while working for a local funeral home. However, his credibility faced significant scrutiny after researchers discovered he had provided a fictitious name for a key witness in his account. Despite this, some proponents note that Dennis had shared consistent details about his experiences long before the Roswell case gained widespread public attention.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glenn_Dennis

item.title: Majestic 12
item.description:
Majestic 12, also known as Majic-12 or MJ-12, is a purported organization that emerged in fake documents circulated by ufologists in 1984. These documents claim it was a secret committee formed in 1947 by President Harry S. Truman to investigate alien spacecraft. The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) has declared these documents “completely bogus,” and many consider them a hoax. Despite this, the concept remains popular in UFO conspiracy theories and has appeared in various forms of popular culture.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Majestic_12

item.title: William L. Moore and His Contributions to UFOlogy
item.description:
William L. Moore, born in 1943, is an author and former UFO researcher known for his work from the late 1970s to the late 1980s. He co-authored books with Charles Berlitz, including “The Philadelphia Experiment - Project Invisibility” and “The Roswell Incident,” which alleged extraterrestrial involvement in the Roswell incident. Moore also circulated the Majestic 12 documents, which purported to reveal a secret government group overseeing UFOs. Critics have described his books as unsubstantiated and his work on the Majestic 12 documents as fraudulent.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bill_Moore_%28ufologist%29

item.title: International UFO Museum and Research Center
item.description:
The International UFO Museum and Research Center is located in Roswell, New Mexico, and was established in 1991 as a nonprofit educational organization. It is largely focused on the 1947 Roswell incident and other UFO events. The museum features exhibits on UFO encounters and an extensive library of related materials. It also serves as a central point for the annual UFOfest held in Roswell.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_UFO_Museum_and_Research_Center
